blocks.content: Claire Hailey did not have a speech prepared.
She stood at the front and looked at the room and said, simply:

blocks.speaker: Claire
blocks.content: She used to fall asleep in the car. Every time. It didn't matter how long the drive was — ten minutes, she was gone. I used to look at her in the rearview mirror.

blocks.speaker: Claire
blocks.content: I took so many pictures of her asleep in car seats. I couldn't stop looking at her. Every time.
She was quiet for a moment.

blocks.speaker: Claire
blocks.content: She made every room she was in better. Not louder. Not bigger. Better. More worth being in. You knew it when she left a room — it changed. Anyone who ever shared space with her knows exactly what I mean.

blocks.speaker: Claire
blocks.content: She is the best thing I ever made.
